Transcrição Completa: 11 - How To Present Your Services
[00:00] So, when you start presenting your services and your offers and you're just wondering how you can display all these different things that you sell and that your strategic referral network sells, I've actually put a lot of what I've used inside of my agency right here. I'll showcase some of these for the first five as an example.
[00:20] So, at one point in my agency, I would use this price sheet here. And this I called my general price sheet. This was something I would hand off to my internal staff and in addition to that I would also use it to obviously give to clients so they had a clear idea of what our services cost.
[00:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "General Price Sheet"
NÚMEROS: 17 membros de equipe
VISUAL: Documento de precificação geral com múltiplos serviços listados, layout organizado mostrando diferentes categorias de serviços e seus respectivos valores
[00:42] At this point in my agency when I leveraged this specific document I had about 17 different staff a part of my services and my overall agency operations. Everything that you see here could have its own pricing sheet or if you specialize in something you want to have that as its own pricing sheet.
[01:00] So, these are things that we would offer kind of on the side or just costs that we wanted to have clearly documented. You know, something that we could use as a visual aid. These are services we really wouldn't prefer to do. That's why I created them on this sheet and kind of lumped them all together.
[01:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Press Release Pricing Sheet"
NÚMEROS: $2,000 (preço de venda), $60 (custo no Fiverr), 15-20% (comissão de vendas), 550 redes diferentes, $20-$50 (preços no Fiverr)
VISUAL: Documento de precificação específico para press releases, mostrando estrutura detalhada do serviço com logos de redes de TV (ABC, NBC, Fox, CBS, MSNBC)
[01:20] Uh, another example of what you guys can use. This is for press releases. So, it was so easy to do and you guys can do the same. It's a great action to repeat as I talked about on uh on fiverr.com. You can type in ABC, NBC, Fox, CBS, and you're going to see all these people here.
[01:38] Look 20 bucks, 50 bucks. Uh, and this and the cost of each one of these are going to vary. I prefer to do the ones that have uh the most rating usually. So, as an example of that, there should be some kind of way to filter filter on here. Top rated seller right here. This person's got 57 reviews. This person's got 26.
[01:58] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Página do Fiverr - Serviço de Press Release
NÚMEROS: 57 reviews, 26 reviews, $60 (custo do serviço)
VISUAL: Interface do Fiverr mostrando vendedores de press release, sistema de classificação com estrelas, filtros de "Top Rated Seller", perfis de vendedores com avaliações
[02:02] And when I click into this, just as an example of where this pricing sheet comes from, it is purely from selling this right here. And you can see we would sell this for $2,000. And it would cost my agency the commission I would pay out to my sales guy, which would be between 15 and 20%. And I could get NBC, CBS, Fox, ABC, and MSNBC articles on 550 different networks guaranteed for 60 bucks.
[02:30] The pricing sheet itself is pretty simple. Although that was the service we were selling, we want to make sure that the client understands the value that they're getting and that we're simply handling the cycle. We obviously would not likely make the client aware of what we're using.
[02:46] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Press Release Package Breakdown"
NÚMEROS: 20 minutos (sessão de estratégia), 5-6 meses (tempo de publicação), $2,000 (preço)
VISUAL: Lista detalhada de bullet points mostrando componentes do serviço: sessão de estratégia, escrita do press release, logos das redes (ABC, NBC, CBS, Fox), benefícios de SEO, layout profissional com marca da agência
[02:50] I broke this down. So, if I handed this to one of my sales staff, they could read it and it'd be as if they're making a pitch. Uh, in addition to that, I wanted the same impact to have u this the same probability of interpretation of, oh, hey, I can understand what this is. I understand how it works if I would hand it to a client as well.
[03:10] So, 20-minute strategy session covering the press release topic. This is necessary anyway when you close a deal to have a conversation with the person of course about what it is. But we would make that conversation be special, calling it a strategy session and going through what they want talked about, what other press they had. And we would really hype it up to make sure that the person had uh great perception on what we were selling even though it was such a simple service to offer.
[03:38] We're going to write the press release for you. So, as you can see here inside of the Fiverr article, they actually include them writing the article and how many keywords it includes. So, can't get better than that, right?
[03:50] You'll be featured in ABC, NBC, CBS, and Fox. So, those are the networks as you can see here. We could even add MSNBC if we're using this particular Fiverr seller. And that's what we would leverage right there.
[04:02] A whole another selling point is that they get to use those logos on the website. So, in addition to what these people offer, they'll tell you, hey, you can use these logos on your website now just from having been published on those pages. So, that's where you can start saying it in their social media profiles, which is another feature that you could add in here.
[04:22] And then in addition to that, you'll get an SEO boost from this. So we would make sure we'd set the right expectation for the client when we would sell this specific bullet point. We'd say, you know, it takes about five to six months for these articles to really publish. And if you're running engagement campaigns, just sending traffic to them on other channels, emailing it to your list if you have a sizable list. That can all spike the probability of these things getting ranked in higher result spots and positions comparatively to if you just have them and you don't run any traffic towards them. So make sure you set that clearly.
[04:56] Then as you can see the price sheet overall is branded for my organization. So my internal staff would have something to model off of and then the external uh clients that would look at it would would feel valued.
[05:08] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Content Distribution Price Sheet"
NÚMEROS: $3,000 (serviço recorrente), 30 minutos (sessão de estratégia), 30 dias (duração do conteúdo)
VISUAL: Documento de precificação para distribuição de conteúdo, layout similar ao press release mas destacando natureza recorrente do serviço, inclui Facebook advertising, relatórios semanais, postagens
[05:10] Content distribution price sheet. This is an incredible asset and it's a service that we sold for $3,000. This would be a recurring service whereas the press release would be a transactional service.
[05:24] So, the content distribution package, and keep in mind the whole intention of this video is I'm showcasing to you the ways that I would show my clients and my staff all of these different services that we sold. So, the ones that we wanted to sell or that I wanted to sell more than everything else, I would make specific pricing sheets for.
[05:44] So, for the content distribution package, it's the same concept. It's a $3,000 recurring service, and usually we'd have people upsell in high frequency to the other services on that general sheet. So, we'd include a 30-minute strategy session, 30 days worth of content. We'd include Facebook advertising, we'd include weekly reports, and we'd post.
[06:08] So, it's quite simple, but nonetheless very effective to use if you're trying to get your point across to your staff, duplicate the knowledge that you have. And this is the same exact concept. One of my sales guys was reading through this. We always start with the price and then we justify the value that comes after the price. So, somebody could read this word for word and they'd have an understanding of exactly what it is and what it means.
[06:32] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Deck - Presentation Slides"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Conjunto de slides de apresentação mostrando processos, serviços, passos e how-to, design profissional para uso em Zoom calls ou impresso, formato de apresentação visual completa
[06:34] Uh, in addition to that, I've had some other things inside of here. The deck. Okay, this was a set of slides and presentations that we would use to show all kinds of cool stuff about what we did. This would be great to use if I'm on a Zoom call and I want to show somebody a process and I'm kind of selling them with a presentation and they're a visual person.
[06:54] This could also be highly effective if you print it off at a local FedEx store, get the little spiralbound um whatever you call it, binder or binding on it. Anyway, long story short, this becomes very effective to use in person and print off. This can also be a great aid to take to an actual uh event.
[07:14] So, long story short, we showcase our processes. We showcase our different services that we offer. We showcase the steps, the how-to, and there's all kinds of cool stuff that we use here. And it looks like it's still downloading a little bit. There's some pieces missing there.
[07:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: Link específico para eventos presenciais
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Material promocional/landing page específica para distribuição em eventos ao vivo, preparado para equipe de vendas entregar na parte de trás da sala após palestras
[07:30] We also have if I go speak at an event, uh this is a service that we offer. We have a specific link to drive traffic to if we go speak in person. And this would be something that I would either hand out, have ready to be handed out or have my sales staff ready for in the back of a room if we would go as an agency trying to attract clients after speaking to a small local event.
[07:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Digital Playbook - First Version"
NÚMEROS: 2-3 parágrafos (baseball card), 3 pessoas no total (equipe inicial), 2 outros membros de equipe
VISUAL: Capa do playbook digital original, design inicial da apresentação, primeira página com branding da agência, formato de documento imprimível com capa plástica transparente
[07:54] Uh, in addition to that, the decks that I would use, and there's big differences between these decks, just to be clear, and you'll see how it evolved through time. So, I'll start with my original deck that I used, and then I'll show you how it evolved. This was the first page here. It's called the digital playbook. Same concept, okay?
[08:14] You could print this thing off. Uh you could do the FedEx Kinkos spiral bound uh for the binding and then have a nice nice one of plastic clear covers on it. Great to take to a presentation in person. Also great to send to clients so they can get an understanding of who you are and what you do.
[08:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Baseball Card"
NÚMEROS: 2-3 parágrafos de biografia
VISUAL: Página de perfil pessoal estilo "baseball card" com foto, biografia curta (2-3 parágrafos), estatísticas impressionantes destacadas, layout profissional para construção de autoridade
[08:32] Here I called this the baseball card. I would leverage a small set of maybe two to three paragraphs about yourself, what you've done. And you could see here at the time this is how I was marketing myself and what I would do. I made sure that all the statistics that we had that were very greatl looking were leveraged right here on the baseball card just like any other base baseball card would be.
[08:54] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Process Breakdown - Funnel Visualization"
NÚMEROS: Passos do processo A para B
VISUAL: Diagrama de processo mostrando etapas para gerar resultados, inspirado no digitalmarketer.com mas recriado para evitar copyright, visualização de funis contextuais de marketing, fluxograma detalhado
[08:56] Uh then I broke down processes that we use in my agency. So I would explain the steps that we take to generate people from A to B a set of results. In addition to that, this comes from digitalmarketer.com. I had it recreated, changed the terms that we wouldn't have any copyright infringement and used something a little more advanced that way people could see how our funnels worked and how contextual we got inside of our marketing campaigns.
[09:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "First Pricing Structure - Social Media Package"
NÚMEROS: $1,800 (preço inicial mais baixo), 2 clientes, 3 pessoas na equipe total, 3+ anos atrás
VISUAL: Primeira estrutura de precificação da agência, pacote de redes sociais detalhado com bullet points, número de posts por canal especificado, ícone de megalodon marcando diferentes níveis de serviço
[09:24] Now, this is where we get into pricing and this is where I say that you can use this as a visual aid to showcase how much you charge for what. This was the very first digital playbook and set of services that I offered. So, this was the lowest pricing that I offered throughout my agency.
[09:42] And I want to be clear, this was a comfortable starting point for me. And I quickly realized with only two other staff members, so three people in total, that we had to raise our pricing after only two clients getting this particular service.
[09:56] But to be clear, we would show the price, we would explain what the service is, and this was a social media package. We would show the bullet points, and this was over three years ago we were using this, if not if not longer. And then we would explain how many posts we would make on each specific channel.
[10:14] Uh we would then roll over to the different services we offered. As you can see going down here on the left, it's really just different advertising services if we would build funnels for people or not and just, you know, emails, copywriting, etc.
[10:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Service Tiers with Megalodon Icons"
NÚMEROS: $3K, $6K, $10K (diferentes níveis de serviço)
VISUAL: Diferentes serviços de publicidade listados verticalmente, ícones de megalodon marcando cada nível, diferenciação visual clara entre pacotes, inclui construção de funis, emails, copywriting
[10:30] So, the differences in cost here were quite simple and we marked them with a little icon that represented a megalodon for my agency. So, in addition to that, I always made sure that I had it known that I was a speaker even though at the time I didn't speak even one time publicly.
[10:48] I just made sure I wanted to sell that and have people who I'm giving this to have the impression that I'm speaking even if I'm just selling speaking. So, long story short, it builds authority that way. This is a very simple playbook.
[11:02] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Digital Playbook - Fifth Evolution"
NÚMEROS: $4K (novo preço do pacote social media, antes $1,800), $12K (preço all-inclusive, antes $3K/$6K/$10K separados), 5ª evolução do documento
VISUAL: Versão evoluída do playbook, foto do baseball card atualizada, parágrafos modificados, estatísticas mantidas, processos iguais, estrutura de precificação simplificada para modelo all-or-nothing
[11:04] And what it evolved into, here's a few evolutions later. This was the fifth evolution. Okay, my baseball card photo evolved a little bit. The paragraphs that I had in here changed. The statistics stayed the same. The processes stayed the same. Pricing changed.
[11:22] So, instead of 1,800, we scaled it up to 4K. Everything else stayed the same. In addition to that, instead of charging 3K, 6K or 10K for all those services that I mentioned, it became an all or nothing thing. It became, hey, we'll just do all of these services for you that you see here for 12K flat and that's it.
[11:44] Sometimes we would take clients a little bit below our pricing, but I would never recommend that you go below your pricing. And of course, we just kept the the speaking keynote.
[11:54] Long story short, there's all kinds of different ways that you guys can be showcasing your services, your pricing, and your processes. So, to be clear, uh, in the next video, we're going to go over ways that you can demonstrate yourself with visual aids and the steps to your processes using some of the PDFs and things inside of here that you can download. I'll see you in the next video.
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
17 membros de equipe na agência no auge
Press Release: $2,000 (venda) vs $60 (custo Fiverr) = margem de ~96%
Comissão de vendas: 15-20%
550 redes diferentes de distribuição de press release
Tempo de publicação de press releases: 5-6 meses
Content Distribution Package: $3,000 recorrente
Sessões de estratégia: 20-30 minutos
Conteúdo: 30 dias de material
Evolução de precificação Social Media: $1,800 → $4,000 (aumento de 122%)
Evolução de precificação All-Inclusive: $3K-$10K separados → $12K flat
3+ anos de operação documentada
5 evoluções do Digital Playbook
Equipe inicial: 3 pessoas total (2 staff + proprietário)
Crescimento após apenas 2 clientes iniciais
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Sistema de Pricing Sheets:
General Price Sheet (para equipe interna e clientes)
Pricing Sheets específicos para serviços prioritários
Serviços secundários agrupados em uma única sheet
Estratégia de Arbitragem Fiverr:
Compra de serviços de press release no Fiverr ($60)
Revenda com markup de 3,233% ($2,000)
Foco em vendedores "Top Rated" com mais reviews
Inclusão de logos de redes (ABC, NBC, CBS, Fox, MSNBC)
Modelo de Serviços:
Transacionais: Press Releases (one-time)
Recorrentes: Content Distribution ($3K/mês)
All-Inclusive: Pacote completo $12K (evolução final)
Sistema de Apresentação em Camadas:
General Price Sheet (overview geral)
Specific Price Sheets (serviços prioritários)
The Deck (apresentações visuais)
Digital Playbook (documento completo imprimível)
Estratégia de Valor Percebido:
Sempre mostrar preço primeiro
Justificar valor depois
Incluir "sessões de estratégia" para elevar percepção
Destacar benefícios de branding (uso de logos)
Enfatizar benefícios de SEO com expectativas realistas
Sistema de Distribuição de Materiais:
Digital (Zoom calls, emails)
Impresso (FedEx Kinkos, spiral bound, capa plástica)
Eventos ao vivo (material na parte de trás da sala)
Links específicos para eventos
Estratégia de Autoridade:
"Baseball Card" com estatísticas impressionantes
Posicionamento como palestrante (mesmo sem experiência inicial)
Branding consistente em todos os materiais
Evolução visual ao longo do tempo
Sistema de Upsell:
Começar com serviço recorrente ($3K)
Fazer upsell frequente para serviços do general sheet
Evolução para modelo all-inclusive ($12K)
ESTRUTURA:
Hierarquia de Documentos:
Nível 1: General Price Sheet (visão geral)
Nível 2: Specific Service Sheets (press release, content distribution)
Nível 3: The Deck (apresentações visuais)
Nível 4: Digital Playbook (documento completo)
Anatomia do Pricing Sheet:
Preço em destaque (primeiro elemento)
Justificativa de valor (bullet points)
Componentes do serviço detalhados
Branding da agência
Layout profissional para duplo uso (staff + clientes)
Estrutura do Digital Playbook:
Capa com título
Baseball Card (biografia + estatísticas)
Process Breakdown (funis e fluxos)
Pricing Structure (serviços e valores)
Speaker/Keynote section (autoridade)
Componentes do Press Release Package:
Sessão de estratégia (20 min)
Escrita do press release
Publicação em redes principais (ABC, NBC, CBS, Fox)
Direito de uso de logos
Benefícios de SEO (5-6 meses)
Componentes do Content Distribution Package:
Sessão de estratégia (30 min)
30 dias de conteúdo
Facebook Advertising
Relatórios semanais
Postagens gerenciadas
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Análise de Margens:
Press Release:
Custo: $60
Venda: $2,000
Comissão (20%): $400
Lucro líquido: $1,540
Margem líquida: 77%
Evolução de Pricing:
Social Media Package:
Versão 1: $1,800
Versão 5: $4,000
Aumento: +122%
Modelo All-Inclusive:
Antes: $3K-$10K (serviços separados)
Depois: $12K flat
Simplificação + aumento de ticket médio
Modelo de Receita:
Transacional: Press Release ($2K one-time)
Recorrente: Content Distribution ($3K/mês)
All-Inclusive: Pacote completo ($12K)
Gatilhos de Crescimento:
Apenas 2 clientes iniciais geraram necessidade de aumento de preços
Crescimento de 3 pessoas → 17 pessoas na equipe
Múltiplas evoluções de pricing (5 versões documentadas)
Investimento em Materiais:
Impressão FedEx Kinkos (spiral bound + capa plástica)
Custo não especificado mas apresentado como acessível
ROI alto através de fechamento de contratos de $3K-$12K
DURAÇÃO: ~12:00 minutos FORMATO: Screencast com demonstração de documentos + explicação verbal
